title: penstemon stenophyllus var. dasyphyllus
intro: Penstemon stenophyllus var. dasyphyllus, also known as Rocky Mountain Beardtongue, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Penstemon stenophyllus var. dasyphyllus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water or using it in a tincture or salve, which can be used topically to treat various skin conditions and wounds.
